text_original: The ancients who showed their skill in practising the Dao did so, not to enlighten the people, but rather to make them simple and ignorant.
text_normalized: The ancients who showed their skill in practising the Dao did so, not to enlighten the people, but rather to make them simple and ignorant.

text_original: The difficulty in governing the people arises from their having much knowledge.
text_normalized: The difficulty in governing the people arises from their having much knowledge.

text_original: He who (tries to) govern a state by his wisdom is a scourge to it; while he who does not (try to) do so is a blessing.
text_normalized: He who (tries to) govern a state by his wisdom is a scourge to it; while he who does not (try to) do so is a blessing.

text_original: He who knows these two things finds in them also his model and rule.
text_normalized: He who knows these two things finds in them also his model and rule.

text_original: Ability to know this model and rule constitutes what we call the mysterious excellence (of a governor).
text_normalized: Ability to know this model and rule constitutes what we call the mysterious excellence (of a governor).

text_original: Deep and far-reaching is such mysterious excellence, showing indeed its possessor as opposite to others, but leading them to a great conformity to him.
text_normalized: Deep and far-reaching is such mysterious excellence, showing indeed its possessor as opposite to others, but leading them to a great conformity to him.
